messages.content:
{
  "analysis": "The primary failure is the 'Names after von' rule matching months (November, Oktober) and legal terms (Familienbeihilfe) as persons. The user feedback explicitly requests splitting this into two cases: 1) 'von' + Title + Name (keep as is), and 2) 'von' + No Title + Name (require exactly two capitalized words). This prevents matching single-word months or terms. Additionally, the 'Names after Zu' rule needs to be tightened to require a colon after the name, as per user feedback. Finally, the system is missing rules to capture names with academic titles (like 'Bakk. techn.') appearing in contexts like 'Frau [Name], [Title]' or standalone '[Name], [Title]', which are failing in the provided examples.",
  "strategy": "1. Replace the 'Names after von' rule with two specific rules: one for 'von' + Title + Name (preserving the original logic) and one for 'von' + First Name + Last Name (requiring exactly two capitalized words to exclude months/terms). 2. Update the 'Names after Zu' rule to enforce the colon requirement. 3. Add a new rule to capture 'Frau [Name], [Title]' patterns. 4. Add a new rule to capture '[Name], [Title]' patterns (e.g., 'Stella Marschalk, Bakk. techn.') to handle the missing entities in the failure cases.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "Names after 'von' (Possessive) with Title",
      "description": "Identifies person names appearing after 'von' with a title. Preserves the original logic for title disambiguation.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\bvon\\s+(?:Dr\\.in(?:\\s+Dr\\.in\\s+Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|\\s+Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|\\s+Dr\\.in)?|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|DDr\\.|Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\.|M\\.B\\.L\\.|LL\\.M\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|StR\\.|RgR\\.|KommR\\.|\\u00d6kR\\.|VetR\\.|OSR\\.|PhD\\.|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Ri|R1|R2|R3|Techn\\s+R\\s+OMedR|Techn\\s+R\\s+HR|Techn\\s+R)?([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+(?:von|de|la|del|di|da|van|ter|in\\s+der)?[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*)",
      "priority": 9,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Handles 'von' + Title + Name cases where the title provides sufficient disambiguation."
    },
    {
      "name": "Names after 'von' (Possessive) without Title",
      "description": "Identifies person names appearing after 'von' without a title. Requires exactly two capitalized words (First Last) to avoid matching months or legal terms.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\bvon\\s+([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)(?!\\s+(?:ung|heit|keit|schaft|t\\u00e4t|e|n)\\b)(?!\\s*(?:Anspruch|Zinsen|Geb\\u00fchren|Frist|Verfahren|Sache|Recht|Klage|Urteil|Beschluss|Verf\\u00fcgung|Entscheidung|Einsatzgeb\\u00fchren|Familienbeihilfe|Kinderabsetzbetrag|Bescheide|Verfahren|Sache|Recht|Klage|Urteil|Beschluss|Verf\\u00fcgung|Entscheidung|Verfahren|Sache|Recht|Klage|Urteil|Beschluss|Verf\\u00fcgung|Entscheidung)\\b)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Replaces the broad single-word match with a strict two-word requirement to prevent false positives like 'von November' or 'von Familienbeihilfe'."
    },
    {
      "name": "Names after 'Zu' (To) with Colon",
      "description": "Identifies person names appearing after 'Zu' in legal contexts, requiring a colon directly after the name.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\bZu\\s+(?!den|die|dem|der|den\\s+Akten|den\\s+Verfahren|den\\s+Sachen)([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)\\s*:",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Tightens the 'Zu' rule by requiring a colon after the name, as requested in user feedback."
    },
    {
      "name": "Frau [Name], [Title]",
      "description": "Captures female names followed by academic titles in the format 'Frau [Name], [Title]'.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "Frau\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)\\s*,\\s*(?:Bakk\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.|Mag\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Ri\\.|StR\\.|RgR\\.|KommR\\.|\\u00d6kR\\.|VetR\\.|OSR\\.|PhD\\.|M\\.B\\.L\\.|LL\\.M\\.)",
      "priority": 7,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Addresses failures where 'Frau Stella Marschalk, Bakk. techn.' was only partially matched or missed."
    },
    {
      "name": "[Name], [Title]",
      "description": "Captures names followed by academic titles in the format '[Name], [Title]' (e.g., 'Stella Marschalk, Bakk. techn.').",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)\\s*,\\s*(?:Bakk\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.|Mag\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Ri\\.|StR\\.|RgR\\.|KommR\\.|\\u00d6kR\\.|VetR\\.|OSR\\.|PhD\\.|M\\.B\\.L\\.|LL\\.M\\.)",
      "priority": 7,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Addresses failures where names with titles like 'Stella Marschalk, Bakk. techn.' were missed entirely."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": [
    "Names after 'von' (Possessive)",
    "Names after 'Zu' (To)"
  ]
}

{
  "analysis": "The primary failure is the 'Names after von' rule matching months (November, Oktober) and legal terms (Familienbeihilfe) as persons. The user feedback explicitly states the rule is too broad and requires a title (e.g., Dr., Mag., Bakk.) to follow 'von'. Additionally, the 'Names after Zu' rule is too broad and needs a colon constraint. Several failures show that names with titles like 'Bakk. techn.' are being missed or partially matched (e.g., 'Stella Marschalk' instead of 'Stella Marschalk, Bakk. techn.'). The current rules rely on 'Herr/Frau' or 'Zu' which are insufficient for the 'Bakk. techn.' pattern found in the data.",
  "strategy": "1. Replace 'Names after von' with a stricter pattern that requires a title immediately after 'von' (e.g., Dr., Mag., Bakk., etc.) to avoid matching months or nouns. 2. Update 'Names after Zu' to require a colon immediately following the name. 3. Add a new rule to capture names followed by academic/professional titles (like 'Bakk. techn.') to fix the partial matches and missed entities in the failure cases. 4. Ensure the new rules use capture groups correctly to extract the full name + title span.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "Names after 'von' (Possessive)",
      "description": "Identifies person names appearing after 'von' (of/from) in legal texts, capturing the full name including title if present. Requires a title to avoid matching months or legal terms.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\bvon\\s+(?:Dr\\.in(?:\\s+Dr\\.in\\s+Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|\\s+Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|\\s+Dr\\.in)?|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|DDr\\.|Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\.|M\\.B\\.L\\.|LL\\.M\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|StR\\.|RgR\\.|KommR\\.|\\u00d6kR\\.|VetR\\.|OSR\\.|PhD\\.|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Ri|R1|R2|R3|Techn\\s+R\\s+OMedR|Techn\\s+R\\s+HR|Techn\\s+R)?([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+(?:von|de|la|del|di|da|van|ter|in\\s+der)?[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s*,\\s*(?:Bakk\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dipl\\.\\s+Ing\\.|Dr\\.\\s+techn\\.|Mag\\.\\s+techn\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.\\s+Dr\\.\\s+techn\\.|Prof\\.\\s+Dr\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+med\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+jur\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+phil\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+ing\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+agr\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+vet\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+forst\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+landw\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+arch\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+kunst\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+mus\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+theol\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+phil\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+jur\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+med\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+agr\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+vet\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+forst\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+landw\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+arch\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+kunst\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+mus\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+theol\\.\\s+techn\\.)?))",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "User feedback indicated false positives on months and legal terms. This rule now strictly requires a title (Dr., Mag., Bakk., etc.) immediately after 'von' to ensure the following text is a person's name."
    },
    {
      "name": "Names after 'Zu' (To)",
      "description": "Identifies person names appearing after 'Zu' (To) in legal contexts, often used to introduce a specific person in a list or context. Requires a colon directly after the name.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\bZu\\s+(?!den|die|dem|der|den\\s+Akten|den\\s+Verfahren|den\\s+Sachen)([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)(?=\\s*:)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "User feedback requested requiring a colon after the name to reduce false positives."
    },
    {
      "name": "Names with Academic Titles",
      "description": "Captures person names followed by academic or professional titles (e.g., Bakk. techn., Mag., Dr.) to ensure full name extraction in contexts like 'Stella Marschalk, Bakk. techn.'",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\b([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)(?:\\s*,\\s*(?:Bakk\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dipl\\.\\s+Ing\\.|Dr\\.\\s+techn\\.|Mag\\.\\s+techn\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.\\s+Dr\\.\\s+techn\\.|Prof\\.\\s+Dr\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+med\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+jur\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+phil\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+ing\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+agr\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+vet\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+forst\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+landw\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+arch\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+kunst\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+mus\\.\\s+techn\\.|Dr\\.\\s+theol\\.\\s+techn\\.)\\b)",
      "priority": 7,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Fixes failures where names like 'Stella Marschalk, Bakk. techn.' were partially matched or missed. This rule captures the full name and title combination."
    },
    {
      "name": "Names with Special Titles",
      "description": "Captures person names preceded by specific titles like Priv.-Doz., Hon.-Prof., etc.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\b(?:Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|DDr\\.|Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\.|M\\.B\\.L\\.|LL\\.M\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|StR\\.|RgR\\.|KommR\\.|\\u00d6kR\\.|VetR\\.|OSR\\.|PhD\\.|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Ri|R1|R2|R3|Techn\\s+R\\s+OMedR|Techn\\s+R\\s+HR|Techn\\s+R)\\s+([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+(?:von|de|la|del|di|da|van|ter|in\\s+der)?[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*)",
      "priority": 9,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Ensures titles like 'Priv.-Doz. Hon.-Prof. Gotthard Clement' are captured as a single entity."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": [
    "Names after 'von' (Possessive)",
    "Names after 'Zu' (To)"
  ]
}
